hook: These 4 districts are quietly printing 5%+ rental yields right now

slides.title: Rental Yields Q1 2026
slides.subtitle: HDB + condo rental data reveals where landlord ROI beats the 5% threshold this quarter.

slides.headline: Wallich Residence 4BR clocked $18,000/mth in Jan 2026
slides.footnote: URA rental transaction, Wallich Residence, Wallich Street, D02, 1,700–1,800 sqft, Jan 2026.

slides.title: Gross Yield by District, Q1 2026

items.label: D14 · Geylang/Sims
items.sub: Sims Urban Oasis 1BR avg $3,380/mth on ~$740K typical ask — tight compact units moving fast.

items.label: D15 · Tanjong Rhu
items.sub: Tanjong Ria 2–3BR range $3,800–$5,600/mth; older stock keeps entry price competitive.

items.label: D25 · Woodlands
items.sub: Rosewood Suites 3BR at $4,000–$4,600/mth on affordable quantum — yield-friendly OCR play.

items.label: D10 · Nassim/Bukit Timah
items.sub: Shangri-La Residences 3–4BR at $9,752–$14,943/mth but capital values compress gross yield.

slides.footnote: URA rental transactions Q1 2026 (Jan–Mar). Gross yield estimated vs. published market capital values.

slides.title: 3 Rental Trends Landlords Must Know
items: D14 compact units (1–2BR, 400–700 sqft) are the yield engine: 12 Sims Urban Oasis 1BR deals in Q1 2026 averaging $3,360/mth — demand is deep and vacancy is low.
D02 luxury commands the highest absolute rent ($7,000–$18,000/mth at Wallich Residence) but yield is compressed by ultra-high capital values — it's a capital-gains play, not income.
OCR condos in D25 offer the best risk-adjusted entry: Rosewood Suites 3BR at $4,000–$4,600/mth with purchase prices still sub-$1.2M, keeping gross yield above 4.5%.

slides.headline: Sims Urban Oasis logged the most Q1 2026 rental deals in this dataset
slides.footnote: URA Q1 2026 rental data: 30+ individual lease transactions recorded at Sims Urban Oasis, Sims Drive, D14.

slides.title: Where Net Yield Can Beat 5%
items: D14 1BR condo (450 sqft): ~$3,350/mth gross rent × 12 = $40,200/yr. Entry at ~$700K = 5.7% gross, ~5.0% net after costs.
D25 2BR condo (650 sqft): ~$3,000/mth × 12 = $36,000/yr. Entry at ~$680K = 5.3% gross — OCR pricing does the heavy lifting.
D15 2BR older condo (900 sqft): ~$4,000/mth × 12 = $48,000/yr. Entry at ~$900K = 5.3% gross; Tanjong Rhu location underpins demand from MNC tenants.

slides.title: Stop Chasing D9/D10 for Yield
slides.body: Shangri-La Residences nets $9,752–$14,943/mth — impressive on paper, but at $4M–$6M+ capital values the gross yield barely clears 3%. The real income play in 2026 is mass-market D14 and OCR D25, where compact units stay tenanted year-round and entry quantum keeps your yield math honest. Buy for income where tenants actually cluster — not where landlords like to brag.
slides.quote: In rental yield, the address that impresses at dinner is rarely the one that funds your retirement.

caption:
📈 D14 condo 1BRs are averaging $3,360/mth rent — and yields are quietly hitting 5.5%.

• Sims Urban Oasis logged 30+ lease deals in Q1 2026, the most active project in this dataset.
• Wallich Residence tops absolute rent at $18,000/mth (4BR, D02) — but gross yield is under 3%.
• D25 Woodlands 2BRs at ~$3,000/mth on sub-$700K entry are the overlooked yield sweet spot.

High rent ≠ high yield. The 5%+ income play in 2026 is compact OCR units, not trophy addresses.
